description: Add missing SDK utility functions to the utilities reference page: postForm, postFormData, httpStatusToToolError, and parseRetryAfterMs.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip && bun run format:check

userStories.title: Document postForm and postFormData fetch helpers
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want documentation for the postForm and postFormData utilities so that I can submit forms and upload files from my plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: The SDK utilities page has a 'postForm' section documenting its signature, parameters, and behavior (POST with URL-encoded form body)
The SDK utilities page has a 'postFormData' section documenting its signature, parameters, and behavior (POST with multipart/form-data)
Both functions show the Zod schema overload (typed response when schema provided)
The fetch utilities summary table includes postForm and postFormData
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx

Source: platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts

postForm signature:
  <T extends z.ZodType>(url: string, body: Record<string, string>, init: FetchFromPageOptions | undefined, schema: T): Promise<z.infer<T>>
  <T>(url: string, body: Record<string, string>, init?: FetchFromPageOptions): Promise<T>
Behavior: POST with Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded. Body is a Record<string, string> of key-value pairs.

postFormData signature:
  <T extends z.ZodType>(url: string, body: FormData, init: FetchFromPageOptions | undefined, schema: T): Promise<z.infer<T>>
  <T>(url: string, body: FormData, init?: FetchFromPageOptions): Promise<T>
Behavior: POST with multipart/form-data (browser sets Content-Type automatically). Body is a FormData object.

Both are re-exported from index.ts. Follow the same documentation pattern as the existing postJSON, putJSON, patchJSON, deleteJSON sections on the same page. Add them in the Fetch Helpers section alongside the other HTTP method helpers.

userStories.title: Document httpStatusToToolError and parseRetryAfterMs utilities
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want documentation for httpStatusToToolError and parseRetryAfterMs so that I can properly handle HTTP errors and rate limiting in my plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: The SDK utilities page has an 'httpStatusToToolError' section documenting its signature (response: Response, message: string): ToolError, and how it maps HTTP status codes to ToolError categories
The SDK utilities page has a 'parseRetryAfterMs' section documenting its signature (value: string): number | undefined, and that it handles both numeric seconds and HTTP-date formats
Both functions are listed in the import example at the top of the page
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx

Source: platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts

httpStatusToToolError signature:
  (response: Response, message: string): ToolError
Behavior: Maps HTTP status codes to ToolError with appropriate categories and retryability. For example: 401/403 → auth, 404 → not_found, 429 → rate_limit (retryable, parses Retry-After header), 408 → timeout (retryable), 5xx → internal.

parseRetryAfterMs signature:
  (value: string): number | undefined
Behavior: Parses the HTTP Retry-After header value. Accepts two formats: numeric (seconds, converted to ms) or HTTP-date (e.g., 'Wed, 21 Oct 2015 07:28:00 GMT'). Returns the delay in milliseconds, or undefined if the value cannot be parsed.

Both are re-exported from index.ts. Add these in a new 'HTTP Error Handling' subsection within the Fetch Helpers section, or as standalone entries after the fetch method helpers. These are lower-level utilities that plugin developers may need when doing custom HTTP requests outside the fetchJSON/postJSON helpers.
